provenance.title: Asset Pricing in Pre-trained Transformers
affiliations: Department of Economics and Related Studies, University of York, Heslington, York, YO10 5DD, UK
provenance.abstract: This paper proposes an innovative Transformer model, Single-directional representative from Transformer (SERT), for US large capital stock pricing. It also innovatively applies pre-trained Transformer models under the stock pricing and factor investment context, compared with standard Transformer and encoder-only Transformer models across three periods covering COVID-19.
key_claims: Proposed SERT and MLP-autoencoder pre-trained Transformer models achieve the highest out-of-sample R2 (11.94% and 11.47%) during extreme market fluctuations, outperforming standard/encoder-only Transformer benchmarks
Trend-following strategies built on the proposed models achieve Sortino ratios ~47% (equal-weighted) and ~28% (value-weighted) higher than buy-and-hold during the pandemic period
Softmax trading-signal filtering removes cross-model performance differences without improving strategy-wise performance
Increasing attention heads improves performance only marginally, and layer-normalization-first (LNF) does not improve performance in this setting

modules.name: InputEmbedding
modules.notes: Linear embedding substitutes Word2Vec-style embedding for numeric factor inputs.

modules.name: MLPAutoencoderPretrain

parameters.role: denoise + dimension-expand to align with decoder/cross-attn dim

modules.notes: Pre-training MLP autoencoder projects 182 sorted-portfolio factors to 420-dim latent space (aligned to number of stocks / cross-attention output dim), analogous to a generalized non-linear PCA. Present only in Pre-trained Transformer (Group 2) and SERT (Group 2) models, absent from Group 3 standard benchmarks.

modules.name: SinusoidalPositionalEncoding
modules.notes: Standard SPE, added (not concatenated) to embedded input: X' = X + PE.

modules.name: MultiHeadSelfAttentionEncoder
modules.notes: Causal mask (upper-triangular -inf) enforced to prevent look-ahead/future leakage; Q,K,V derived via learned linear projections; scaled dot-product attention with mask add before softmax.

modules.name: FeedForwardAutoencoder
modules.notes: Latent-layer neuron count = 70% of input dimension (main-body FFN autoencoder); LNF variant uses 20% of input dimension for the FFN latent size.

modules.name: AddAndLayerNorm
modules.notes: Standard Transformer/pre-trained/SERT groups use post-LN (Add & LayerNorm); LNF variants use pre-LN (LayerNorm first, then Add), per Liu et al. 2020.

modules.name: CrossAttention

parameters.present_in: full Transformer (Pre-trained Transformer, LNF Pre-trained Transformer, standard C_Trans benchmarks) only; absent in encoder-only SERT/En_Trans

modules.notes: Q from decoder output, K/V from encoder output; bridges encoder-decoder; masked cross-attention with M_cross mask.

modules.name: OutputDenseLayer

parameters.activation: none (linear, replaces original softmax layer for direct OOS return regression)

modules.notes: This study replaces the standard Transformer's terminal softmax classification layer with a linear dense layer for direct OOS return regression; softmax is instead reused separately as a post-hoc trading-signal filter.

modules.name: SERT_Encoder
modules.notes: SERT = BERT-style encoder-only Transformer, but (a) causal-masked self-attention instead of bidirectional, and (b) MLP-autoencoder pre-training module instead of random-masking pretraining. Feedforward autoencoder of encoder connects directly (linearly) to output dense layer -- no decoder/cross-attention block.

mathematical_spec.name: QKV projection
mathematical_spec.latex: Q = W^Q X,\quad K = W^K X,\quad V = W^V X

mathematical_spec.name: Cross-attention QKV
mathematical_spec.latex: Q_{de} = H_{de}W^Q,\quad K_{en} = H_{en}W^K,\quad V_{en} = H_{en}W^V

mathematical_spec.name: Training loss (MSE + L1 regularization)
hyperparameters: lambda (L1 coeff, value not specified in text)

mathematical_spec.name: Adam optimizer update
mathematical_spec.latex: m_l=\beta_1 m_{l-1}+(1-\beta_1)g_l;\ v_l=\beta_2 v_{l-1}+(1-\beta_2)g_l\odot g_l;\ \hat m_l=m_l/(1-\beta_1^l);\ \hat v_l=v_l/(1-\beta_2^l);\ \theta_l=\theta_{l-1}-\eta \hat m_l/(\sqrt{\hat v_l}+\epsilon)

training_pipeline.curriculum: Rolling-window re-estimation: 102-month training window, 30-month validation window (30% of training), model parameters re-estimated every 12 months (step size 12).

datasets.name: 182 firm-characteristic sorted-portfolio factors (Andrew Y.; Zimmermann 2020, Open Source Cross-Sectional Asset Pricing)

evaluation_protocol.compute: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 GPU; PyTorch nn.Transformer; feasible on CPU-only laptop for limited data size
special_conditions: dynamic transaction cost = turnover rate x 20bps for large-cap stocks
softmax trading-signal filter removes worst 50% of monthly trading signals across 420 stocks

implementation_assumptions.assumption: L1 regularization coefficient (lambda) value not given; use small default 1e-5
implementation_assumptions.basis: literature default for light L1 regularization on financial NN models

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Activation function inside MLP autoencoder FFN blocks is ReLU
implementation_assumptions.basis: common practice for Transformer FFN blocks; paper does not explicitly name activation

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Batch size not specified; assume full-batch (all 420 stocks) per training step given monthly-frequency, small-N-per-T panel structure
implementation_assumptions.basis: typical practice in monthly asset-pricing factor-model literature (e.g., Gu et al. 2020) which trains on the full cross-section each period

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Learning-rate schedule: constant LR=0.001 (Adam default from Appendix A), no explicit warmup/decay described
implementation_assumptions.basis: Appendix Algorithm 1 gives fixed eta with no schedule mentioned elsewhere

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Early stopping patience parameter p not numerically specified; assume p=10 epochs
implementation_assumptions.basis: Appendix Algorithm 2 defines early stopping mechanism but not the patience value

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Number of training epochs/max iterations not specified; assume max 200 epochs with early stopping
implementation_assumptions.basis: standard practice for small tabular/panel Transformer models

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Number of encoder/decoder blocks N* = 1 for all examined models (explicitly stated: 'the N is configured to 1')
d_model = 420 (equal to number of stocks, aligning cross-attention/output dimension)
implementation_assumptions.basis: explicit statement: pre-train module projects dimensionality from 182 to 420, 'aligning with the output dimension for the cross-attention layer'

ambiguities.description: Exact layer count / hidden dims of the pre-training MLP autoencoder that projects 182->420 dims is not given numerically, only described qualitatively.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: single hidden layer MLP autoencoder with hidden dim = 70% of max(input,output) dim, consistent with main-body FFN sizing convention

ambiguities.description: L1 regularization coefficient lambda is mentioned ('with an L1 regularization term') but its numeric value is never given in the text or appendix.
Exact list/identity of the 420 large-cap U.S. stocks (tickers) used is not enumerated in the paper text provided.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: S&P 500 large-cap constituents subset filtered to 420 stocks with sufficient factor coverage

ambiguities.description: 'P Trans H5' and 'SERT H5' (5-head variants) are absent from all tables; groups jump from H4 to H6. Not explained whether 5-head was excluded intentionally or omitted.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: 5-head variant intentionally excluded from the head-count sweep (no explanation given)
